attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# WP-026 — MRK-V3-01: 3D-Anchored Comments + Threading + @mention
2	
3	**Branch:** `feat/v3-mrk-v3-01-3d-comments`
4	**Effort:** L — ~2.5 weeks
5	**State:** `now` (dep COL-V3-01 merged)
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Required reading
10	
11	Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.
12	
13	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T3 MRK-V3-01 entry (line 1304)
14	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
15	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/collab/orchestrator.py` — yjs/CRDT session; how subspaces are managed (added by COL-V3-01)
16	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` — existing REST routes; add comment routes here
17	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/collab.ts` — collab types (added by COL-V3-01)
18	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — existing element types; add `Comment` type
19	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/collab/CollabProvider.tsx` — collab context; comment store will live alongside
20	
21	Already merged reference — read as patterns, do not re-apply:
22	- COL-V3-01 (multi-user collab): yjs Y.Doc subspaces, collab orchestrator pattern
23	
24	---
25	
26	## Verbatim MRK-V3-01 tracker entry (§6 T3, line 1304)
27	
28	**MRK-V3-01 — 3D-anchored comments with threading + @mention**
29	
30	**Status.** `now`.
31	**Scope.** A `Comment` is entity-anchored or geometry-anchored. Three anchor modes: **(a)** element-anchored; **(b)** point-anchored (3D world coordinate); **(c)** region-anchored (3D bounding volume). Comments are threadable, support `@mention` with email + in-app notification, and re-pin correctly when the model element moves. Surfaces in plan view + 3D view + sheet view. Resolvable.
32	
33	**Data model.**
34	```ts
35	type Comment = {
36	  id: string;
37	  modelId: string;
38	  threadId: string;
39	  authorId: string;
40	  body: string;
41	  mentions: Array<{ userId: string; offsetMm: number }>;
42	  anchor:
43	    | { kind: 'element'; elementId: string; offsetLocalMm?: { xMm; yMm; zMm } }
44	    | { kind: 'point'; worldMm: { xMm; yMm; zMm } }
45	    | { kind: 'region'; bboxMm: { minMm; maxMm } };
46	  createdAt: number;
47	  resolvedAt?: number;
48	  resolvedBy?: string;
49	};
50	```
51	
52	**Engine.** New `comments` Y.Doc subspace inside the collab session. Element-anchored comments survive geometry edits — when the host element moves, the comment's projected pin moves with it; when deleted, comment becomes "orphaned" with a soft warning chip.
53	
54	**Effort.** L — 2.5 weeks.
55	
56	---
57	
58	## Setup
59	
60	```bash
61	git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
62	git checkout -b feat/v3-mrk-v3-01-3d-comments
63	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
64	python -c "from bim_ai.collab.orchestrator import CollabOrchestrator; print('collab ok')"
65	uv sync && pnpm install
66	```
67	
68	---
69	
70	## Implementation tasks
71	
72	### 1. Data model — Python
73	
74	Add `CommentAnchor` discriminated union and `Comment` Pydantic model. Place in a new file `app/bim_ai/comments.py`:
75	
76	```python
77	from __future__ import annotations
78	from typing import Literal
79	from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field
80	
81	class Vec3Mm(BaseModel):
82	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
83	    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
84	    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")
85	    z_mm: float = Field(alias="zMm")
86	
87	class ElementAnchor(BaseModel):
88	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
89	    kind: Literal["element"] = "element"
90	    element_id: str = Field(alias="elementId")
91	    offset_local_mm: Vec3Mm | None = Field(default=None, alias="offsetLocalMm")
92	
93	class PointAnchor(BaseModel):
94	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
95	    kind: Literal["point"] = "point"
96	    world_mm: Vec3Mm = Field(alias="worldMm")
97	
98	class RegionAnchor(BaseModel):
99	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
100	    kind: Literal["region"] = "region"
101	    min_mm: Vec3Mm = Field(alias="minMm")
102	    max_mm: Vec3Mm = Field(alias="maxMm")
103	
104	CommentAnchor = Annotated[
105	    ElementAnchor | PointAnchor | RegionAnchor,
106	    Field(discriminator="kind")
107	]
108	
109	class Comment(BaseModel):
110	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
111	    id: str
112	    model_id: str = Field(alias="modelId")
113	    thread_id: str = Field(alias="threadId")
114	    author_id: str = Field(alias="authorId")
115	    body: str
116	    anchor: CommentAnchor
117	    created_at: int = Field(alias="createdAt")
118	    resolved_at: int | None = Field(default=None, alias="resolvedAt")
119	    resolved_by: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="resolvedBy")
120	    is_orphaned: bool = Field(default=False, alias="isOrphaned")
121	```
122	
123	### 2. Data model — TypeScript (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)
124	
125	Add the `Comment` type matching the Python model. Add `CommentAnchor` discriminated union. Export both.
126	
127	### 3. REST routes (`routes_api.py`)
128	
129	Add four routes under a new `/models/{model_id}/comments` prefix:
130	
131	- `POST /models/{model_id}/comments` — create a new top-level comment or reply
132	- `GET /models/{model_id}/comments` — list all comments for a model (query param `?threadId=` for replies)
133	- `PATCH /models/{model_id}/comments/{comment_id}/resolve` — mark resolved
134	- `DELETE /models/{model_id}/comments/{comment_id}` — hard delete (admin only gate — for now accept any caller, permission tiers land in WP-030)
135	
136	Store comments in a new SQLAlchemy table `comment_rows` or in a JSONB column on the model row. Use the simplest approach — a JSON-serialised list on the model row is fine for now (COL-V3-02 proper storage comes later).
137	
138	### 4. Orphan tracking helper (`comments.py`)
139	
140	Add a pure function:
141	
142	```python
143	def mark_orphaned_comments(
144	    comments: list[Comment],
145	    deleted_element_ids: set[str],
146	) -> list[Comment]:
147	    """Return a new list with element-anchored comments orphaned when their host was deleted."""
148	    result = []
149	    for c in comments:
150	        if (
151	            c.anchor.kind == "element"
152	            and c.anchor.element_id in deleted_element_ids
153	            and not c.is_orphaned
154	        ):
155	            result.append(c.model_copy(update={"is_orphaned": True}))
156	        else:
157	            result.append(c)
158	    return result
159	```
160	
161	Hook this into the engine's element deletion path so comments are orphaned when their host element is removed.
162	
163	### 5. Frontend — comment store (`packages/web/src/collab/commentStore.ts`)
164	
165	Zustand slice that:
166	- Holds `comments: Comment[]` for the active model.
167	- Exposes `addComment`, `resolveComment`, `deleteComment`.
168	- Syncs with the REST API on mount and after each mutation.
169	
170	### 6. Frontend — `CommentPin` component (`packages/web/src/collab/CommentPin.tsx`)
171	
172	SVG/canvas overlay component:
173	- Renders as a circular pin with author initials at the anchor's projected 2D position.
174	- Resolved comments render at hairline opacity.
175	- Orphaned comments render with an orange "?" glyph.
176	- On click: opens a thread popover (200 ms `--ease-paper` slide).
177	
178	### 7. Frontend — `CommentThread` popover (`packages/web/src/collab/CommentThread.tsx`)
179	
180	Thread popover component:
181	- Shows all replies for a `threadId`.
182	- Input at the bottom for new replies; `Enter` submits.
183	- "Resolve" button at top-right; `Cmd+Enter` resolves + closes.
184	- `@mention` autocomplete: `@` in input → dropdown of workspace members.
185	
186	### 8. Tests (`app/tests/api/test_comments_route.py`)
187	
188	Write tests using `TestClient` with a stub FastAPI app (same pattern as `test_apply_bundle_route.py`). Cover:
189	
190	- `POST /comments` creates a comment and returns it.
191	- `GET /comments` returns all comments for the model.
192	- `POST /comments` with `threadId` set creates a reply.
193	- `PATCH /comments/{id}/resolve` marks `resolvedAt`.
194	- `DELETE /comments/{id}` removes the comment.
195	- `mark_orphaned_comments` pure function test: orphans element-anchored comment when element deleted.
196	
197	---
198	
199	## Implementation rules
200	
201	- **yjs subspace.** The `comments` subspace inside the collab Y.Doc is the source of truth for real-time; REST is the persistence/load path. Do not duplicate — the REST layer materialises from yjs on reads if yjs is the canonical store, or vice versa. Choose the simpler path: REST is canonical for v3, yjs is a future enhancement.
202	- **Thread vs top-level.** Top-level comments have `threadId == id`. Replies have `threadId == parent.id`. Validate this invariant on create.
203	- **No hex literals.** CSS token variables only in any new TSX.
204	- **No new element kinds.** `Comment` is not a kernel element — it lives in a separate collection, not in `doc.elements`.
205	
206	---
207	
208	## Verify gate
209	
210	```bash
211	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app
212	python -m pytest tests/api/test_comments_route.py -v
213	python -m pytest tests/ -x -q
214	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
215	pnpm tsc --noEmit
216	pnpm format:check
217	```
218	
219	---
220	
221	## Commit and push
222	
223	```bash
224	git add app/bim_ai/comments.py \
225	        app/bim_ai/routes_api.py \
226	        app/tests/api/test_comments_route.py \
227	        packages/core/src/index.ts \
228	        packages/web/src/collab/commentStore.ts \
229	        packages/web/src/collab/CommentPin.tsx \
230	        packages/web/src/collab/CommentThread.tsx
231	git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
232	feat(mrk): MRK-V3-01 3D-anchored comments — threading + @mention + orphan tracking
233	
234	Comment model with element/point/region anchors; REST CRUD routes; mark_orphaned_comments
235	pure helper; CommentPin + CommentThread frontend components; zustand comment store.
236	
237	Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
238	EOF
239	)"
240	git push -u origin feat/v3-mrk-v3-01-3d-comments
241	```
242	
243	---
244	
245	## Final report template
246	
247	```
248	WP: WP-026 / MRK-V3-01
249	Branch: feat/v3-mrk-v3-01-3d-comments
250	Status: done
251	Tests: <N> passed (test_comments_route.py)
252	Files changed:
253	  - app/bim_ai/comments.py              (Comment model, mark_orphaned_comments)
254	  - app/bim_ai/routes_api.py            (4 comment routes)
255	  - app/tests/api/test_comments_route.py
256	  - packages/core/src/index.ts          (Comment + CommentAnchor types)
257	  - packages/web/src/collab/commentStore.ts
258	  - packages/web/src/collab/CommentPin.tsx
259	  - packages/web/src/collab/CommentThread.tsx
260	Notes: <anything unexpected>

# WP-026 — MRK-V3-01: 3D-Anchored Comments + Threading + @mention

**Branch:** `feat/v3-mrk-v3-01-3d-comments`
**Effort:** L — ~2.5 weeks
**State:** `now` (dep COL-V3-01 merged)

---

## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T3 MRK-V3-01 entry (line 1304)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/collab/orchestrator.py` — yjs/CRDT session; how subspaces are managed (added by COL-V3-01)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` — existing REST routes; add comment routes here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/collab.ts` — collab types (added by COL-V3-01)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — existing element types; add `Comment` type
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/collab/CollabProvider.tsx` — collab context; comment store will live alongside

Already merged reference — read as patterns, do not re-apply:
- COL-V3-01 (multi-user collab): yjs Y.Doc subspaces, collab orchestrator pattern

---

## Verbatim MRK-V3-01 tracker entry (§6 T3, line 1304)

**MRK-V3-01 — 3D-anchored comments with threading + @mention**

**Status.** `now`.
**Scope.** A `Comment` is entity-anchored or geometry-anchored. Three anchor modes: **(a)** element-anchored; **(b)** point-anchored (3D world coordinate); **(c)** region-anchored (3D bounding volume). Comments are threadable, support `@mention` with email + in-app notification, and re-pin correctly when the model element moves. Surfaces in plan view + 3D view + sheet view. Resolvable.

**Data model.**
```ts
type Comment = {
  id: string;
  modelId: string;
  threadId: string;
  authorId: string;
  body: string;
  mentions: Array<{ userId: string; offsetMm: number }>;
  anchor:
    | { kind: 'element'; elementId: string; offsetLocalMm?: { xMm; yMm; zMm } }
    | { kind: 'point'; worldMm: { xMm; yMm; zMm } }
    | { kind: 'region'; bboxMm: { minMm; maxMm } };
  createdAt: number;
  resolvedAt?: number;
  resolvedBy?: string;
};
```

**Engine.** New `comments` Y.Doc subspace inside the collab session. Element-anchored comments survive geometry edits — when the host element moves, the comment's projected pin moves with it; when deleted, comment becomes "orphaned" with a soft warning chip.

**Effort.** L — 2.5 weeks.

---

## Setup

```bash
git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-mrk-v3-01-3d-comments
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
python -c "from bim_ai.collab.orchestrator import CollabOrchestrator; print('collab ok')"
uv sync && pnpm install
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. Data model — Python

Add `CommentAnchor` discriminated union and `Comment` Pydantic model. Place in a new file `app/bim_ai/comments.py`:

```python
from __future__ import annotations
from typing import Literal
from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field

class Vec3Mm(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")
    z_mm: float = Field(alias="zMm")

class ElementAnchor(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["element"] = "element"
    element_id: str = Field(alias="elementId")
    offset_local_mm: Vec3Mm | None = Field(default=None, alias="offsetLocalMm")

class PointAnchor(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["point"] = "point"
    world_mm: Vec3Mm = Field(alias="worldMm")

class RegionAnchor(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["region"] = "region"
    min_mm: Vec3Mm = Field(alias="minMm")
    max_mm: Vec3Mm = Field(alias="maxMm")

CommentAnchor = Annotated[
    ElementAnchor | PointAnchor | RegionAnchor,
    Field(discriminator="kind")
]

class Comment(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    id: str
    model_id: str = Field(alias="modelId")
    thread_id: str = Field(alias="threadId")
    author_id: str = Field(alias="authorId")
    body: str
    anchor: CommentAnchor
    created_at: int = Field(alias="createdAt")
    resolved_at: int | None = Field(default=None, alias="resolvedAt")
    resolved_by: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="resolvedBy")
    is_orphaned: bool = Field(default=False, alias="isOrphaned")
```

### 2. Data model — TypeScript (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

Add the `Comment` type matching the Python model. Add `CommentAnchor` discriminated union. Export both.

### 3. REST routes (`routes_api.py`)

Add four routes under a new `/models/{model_id}/comments` prefix:

- `POST /models/{model_id}/comments` — create a new top-level comment or reply
- `GET /models/{model_id}/comments` — list all comments for a model (query param `?threadId=` for replies)
- `PATCH /models/{model_id}/comments/{comment_id}/resolve` — mark resolved
- `DELETE /models/{model_id}/comments/{comment_id}` — hard delete (admin only gate — for now accept any caller, permission tiers land in WP-030)

Store comments in a new SQLAlchemy table `comment_rows` or in a JSONB column on the model row. Use the simplest approach — a JSON-serialised list on the model row is fine for now (COL-V3-02 proper storage comes later).

### 4. Orphan tracking helper (`comments.py`)

Add a pure function:

```python
def mark_orphaned_comments(
    comments: list[Comment],
    deleted_element_ids: set[str],
) -> list[Comment]:
    """Return a new list with element-anchored comments orphaned when their host was deleted."""
    result = []
    for c in comments:
        if (
            c.anchor.kind == "element"
            and c.anchor.element_id in deleted_element_ids
            and not c.is_orphaned
        ):
            result.append(c.model_copy(update={"is_orphaned": True}))
        else:
            result.append(c)
    return result
```

Hook this into the engine's element deletion path so comments are orphaned when their host element is removed.

### 5. Frontend — comment store (`packages/web/src/collab/commentStore.ts`)

Zustand slice that:
- Holds `comments: Comment[]` for the active model.
- Exposes `addComment`, `resolveComment`, `deleteComment`.
- Syncs with the REST API on mount and after each mutation.

### 6. Frontend — `CommentPin` component (`packages/web/src/collab/CommentPin.tsx`)

SVG/canvas overlay component:
- Renders as a circular pin with author initials at the anchor's projected 2D position.
- Resolved comments render at hairline opacity.
- Orphaned comments render with an orange "?" glyph.
- On click: opens a thread popover (200 ms `--ease-paper` slide).

### 7. Frontend — `CommentThread` popover (`packages/web/src/collab/CommentThread.tsx`)

Thread popover component:
- Shows all replies for a `threadId`.
- Input at the bottom for new replies; `Enter` submits.
- "Resolve" button at top-right; `Cmd+Enter` resolves + closes.
- `@mention` autocomplete: `@` in input → dropdown of workspace members.

### 8. Tests (`app/tests/api/test_comments_route.py`)

Write tests using `TestClient` with a stub FastAPI app (same pattern as `test_apply_bundle_route.py`). Cover:

- `POST /comments` creates a comment and returns it.
- `GET /comments` returns all comments for the model.
- `POST /comments` with `threadId` set creates a reply.
- `PATCH /comments/{id}/resolve` marks `resolvedAt`.
- `DELETE /comments/{id}` removes the comment.
- `mark_orphaned_comments` pure function test: orphans element-anchored comment when element deleted.

---

## Implementation rules

- **yjs subspace.** The `comments` subspace inside the collab Y.Doc is the source of truth for real-time; REST is the persistence/load path. Do not duplicate — the REST layer materialises from yjs on reads if yjs is the canonical store, or vice versa. Choose the simpler path: REST is canonical for v3, yjs is a future enhancement.
- **Thread vs top-level.** Top-level comments have `threadId == id`. Replies have `threadId == parent.id`. Validate this invariant on create.
- **No hex literals.** CSS token variables only in any new TSX.
- **No new element kinds.** `Comment` is not a kernel element — it lives in a separate collection, not in `doc.elements`.

---

## Verify gate

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app
python -m pytest tests/api/test_comments_route.py -v
python -m pytest tests/ -x -q
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
pnpm tsc --noEmit
pnpm format:check
```

---

## Commit and push

```bash
git add app/bim_ai/comments.py \
        app/bim_ai/routes_api.py \
        app/tests/api/test_comments_route.py \
        packages/core/src/index.ts \
        packages/web/src/collab/commentStore.ts \
        packages/web/src/collab/CommentPin.tsx \
        packages/web/src/collab/CommentThread.tsx
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(mrk): MRK-V3-01 3D-anchored comments — threading + @mention + orphan tracking

Comment model with element/point/region anchors; REST CRUD routes; mark_orphaned_comments
pure helper; CommentPin + CommentThread frontend components; zustand comment store.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git push -u origin feat/v3-mrk-v3-01-3d-comments
```

1	"""COL-V3-01 — server-side collab orchestrator.
2	
3	Manages one yjs Y-WebSocket room per modelId. Relays raw yjs sync + awareness
4	messages between browser clients. Does NOT interpret CRDT contents — yjs
5	algorithms handle merge deterministically on the client side.
6	
7	# Architecture note
8	yjs is the in-flight transport for pre-commit, live-preview mutations only.
9	The canonical commit boundary remains try_commit_bundle (engine.py). When a
10	user wants to persist changes they POST to /api/models/{model_id}/bundles
11	via CMD-V3-01, the same HTTP route used by the REST API and the agent. yjs
12	state is ephemeral between commits; the server is still authoritative at
13	every commit boundary.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import logging
19	from typing import Any
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	
24	class CollabRoom:
25	    """One room = one model's in-flight CRDT state."""
26	
27	    def __init__(self, model_id: str) -> None:
28	        self.model_id = model_id
29	        self.connections: set[Any] = set()
30	        self.awareness_states: dict[str, dict] = {}
31	
32	    async def broadcast(self, message: bytes, exclude: Any = None) -> None:
33	        """Broadcast a yjs sync message to all connections except sender."""
34	        dead: set[Any] = set()
35	        for conn in self.connections:
36	            if conn is exclude:
37	                continue
38	            try:
39	                await conn.send_bytes(message)
40	            except Exception:
41	                dead.add(conn)
42	        self.connections -= dead
43	
44	    def join(self, conn: Any) -> None:
45	        self.connections.add(conn)
46	
47	    def leave(self, conn: Any) -> None:
48	        self.connections.discard(conn)
49	
50	
51	class CollabOrchestrator:
52	    """Multiplexes collab rooms by model_id."""
53	
54	    def __init__(self) -> None:
55	        self._rooms: dict[str, CollabRoom] = {}
56	
57	    def get_room(self, model_id: str) -> CollabRoom:
58	        if model_id not in self._rooms:
59	            self._rooms[model_id] = CollabRoom(model_id)
60	        return self._rooms[model_id]
61	
62	    def remove_empty_rooms(self) -> None:
63	        empty = [mid for mid, r in self._rooms.items() if not r.connections]
64	        for mid in empty:
65	            del self._rooms[mid]
66	
67	
68	_orchestrator: CollabOrchestrator | None = None
69	
70	
71	def get_orchestrator() -> CollabOrchestrator:
72	    global _orchestrator
73	    if _orchestrator is None:
74	        _orchestrator = CollabOrchestrator()
75	    return _orchestrator
76

287	async def _resolve_link_source_revisions(
288	    session: AsyncSession, host_doc: Document
289	) -> dict[str, int]:
290	    """Look up the current revision of every distinct source UUID referenced
291	    by a ``link_model`` element in ``host_doc``. Missing sources are omitted
292	    from the result. Used by the FED-01 drift-badge UI."""
293	
294	    out: dict[str, int] = {}
295	    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
296	        if not isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
297	            continue
298	        src_uuid_str = elem.source_model_id
299	        if src_uuid_str in out:
300	            continue
301	        try:
302	            src_uuid = UUID(src_uuid_str)
303	        except ValueError:
304	            continue
305	        src_row = await load_model_row(session, src_uuid)
306	        if src_row is None:
307	            continue
308	        try:
309	            src_doc = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
310	        except Exception:
311	            continue
312	        out[src_uuid_str] = int(src_doc.revision)
313	    return out
314	
315	
316	async def _expand_host_links(
317	    session: AsyncSession,
318	    host_doc: Document,
319	    host_elements_wire: dict[str, Any],
320	) -> dict[str, Any]:
321	    """FED-01: resolve every ``link_model`` row's source document from DB and
322	    pass it through ``expand_links`` to inline transformed source elements.
323	
324	    Sources are loaded at their pinned revision when set (replayed via the
325	    undo stack), or at their current revision otherwise. Missing sources are
326	    skipped silently — the host is still authoritative.
327	    """
328	
329	    cache: dict[tuple[str, int | None], Document | None] = {}
330	
331	    async def _load_source(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
332	        cache_key = (source_uuid_str, source_rev)
333	        if cache_key in cache:
334	            return cache[cache_key]
335	        try:
336	            source_uuid = UUID(source_uuid_str)
337	        except ValueError:
338	            cache[cache_key] = None
339	            return None
340	        src_row = await load_model_row(session, source_uuid)
341	        if src_row is None:
342	            cache[cache_key] = None
343	            return None
344	        current = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
345	        if source_rev is None or source_rev == current.revision:
346	            cache[cache_key] = current
347	            return current
348	        # Replay backwards through the undo stack to land at the requested
349	        # revision (mirrors the diff endpoint's logic).
350	        try:
351	            doc_at = await _document_at_revision(session, source_uuid, current, source_rev)
352	        except HTTPException:
353	            cache[cache_key] = None
354	            return None
355	        cache[cache_key] = doc_at
356	        return doc_at
357	
358	    # Pre-load every link's source synchronously (the providers callable in
359	    # ``expand_links`` is sync; we resolve up-front).
360	    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
361	        if isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
362	            await _load_source(elem.source_model_id, elem.source_model_revision)
363	
364	    def _provider(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
365	        return cache.get((source_uuid_str, source_rev))
366	
367	    return expand_links(host_doc, host_elements_wire, _provider)
368	
369	
370	async def _document_at_revision(
371	    session: AsyncSession, model_id: UUID, current: Document, target_rev: int
372	) -> Document:
373	    """Reconstruct ``current`` rolled back to ``target_rev`` by replaying
374	    undo bundles in reverse-revision order. Returns a fresh ``Document``;
375	    the ``revision`` attribute is informational and may not equal
376	    ``target_rev`` after the engine bumps the counter — element state is
377	    what matters for diff.
378	    """
379	    if target_rev == current.revision:
380	        return clone_document(current)
381	    res = await session.execute(
382	        select(UndoStackRecord)
383	        .where(
384	            UndoStackRecord.model_id == model_id,
385	            UndoStackRecord.revision_after > target_rev,
386	            UndoStackRecord.revision_after <= current.revision,
387	        )
388	        .order_by(desc(UndoStackRecord.revision_after), desc(UndoStackRecord.id))
389	    )
390	    rolling = clone_document(current)
391	    for entry in res.scalars().all():
392	        ok, new_doc, _cmds, _viols, _code = try_commit_bundle(rolling, list(entry.undo_commands))
393	        if ok and new_doc is not None:
394	            rolling = new_doc
395	        else:
396	            raise HTTPException(
397	                status_code=409,
398	                detail=(
399	                    "Cannot reconstruct historical revision — undo replay failed at "
400	                    f"revision_after={entry.revision_after}"
401	                ),
402	            )
403	    return rolling
404	
405	
406	@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/diff")
407	async def model_diff(
408	    model_id: UUID,
409	    fromRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803 — wire-format alias
410	    toRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803
411	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
412	) -> dict[str, Any]:
413	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
414	    if row is None:
415	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
416	    current = Document.model_validate(row.document)
417	
418	    to_rev = toRev if toRev is not None else current.revision
419	    from_rev = fromRev if fromRev is not None else max(1, to_rev - 1)
420	
421	    if from_rev > current.revision or to_rev > current.revision:
422	        raise HTTPException(
423	            status_code=400,
424	            detail=(
425	                f"Revision out of range: model is at revision {current.revision} "
426	                f"(fromRev={from_rev}, toRev={to_rev})."
427	            ),
428	        )
429	
430	    doc_to = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, to_rev)
431	    doc_from = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, from_rev)
432	
433	    elements_from = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_from.elements.items()}
434	    elements_to = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_to.elements.items()}
435	
436	    diff = compute_element_diff(elements_from, elements_to)
437	    return {
438	        "modelId": str(model_id),
439	        "fromRevision": from_rev,
440	        "toRevision": to_rev,
441	        **diff,
442	    }

798	    "/models/{model_id}/schedules/{schedule_id}/table",
799	    response_model=None,
800	)
801	async def schedule_derived_table(
802	    model_id: UUID,
803	    schedule_id: str,
804	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
805	    fmt: Annotated[str, Query(alias="format")] = "json",
806	    columns: Annotated[str | None, Query(alias="columns")] = None,
807	    include_schedule_totals_csv: Annotated[bool, Query(alias="includeScheduleTotalsCsv")] = False,
808	) -> dict[str, Any] | PlainTextResponse:
809	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
810	    if row is None:
811	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
812	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
813	    try:
814	        payload = derive_schedule_table(doc, schedule_id)
815	    except ValueError as exc:
816	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from exc
817	    if fmt.strip().lower() == "csv":
818	        export_payload = payload
819	        if columns and columns.strip():
820	            wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
821	            if wanted:
822	                export_payload = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
823	        csv_body = schedule_payload_to_csv(
824	            export_payload,
825	            include_totals_csv=include_schedule_totals_csv,
826	        )
827	        safe = "".join(ch for ch in schedule_id if ch.isalnum() or ch in ("-", "_")) or "schedule"
828	        return PlainTextResponse(
829	            csv_body,
830	            media_type="text/csv; charset=utf-8",
831	            headers={"Content-Disposition": f'attachment; filename="{safe}.csv"'},
832	        )
833	    out = payload
834	    if columns and columns.strip():
835	        wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
836	        if wanted:
837	            out = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
838	    return out

862	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
863	# FED-04 — IFC → shadow-model link import
864	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
865	
866	
867	class ImportIfcBody(BaseModel):
868	    """FED-04: payload for ``POST /api/models/{host_id}/import-ifc``.
869	
870	    Either ``file_text`` (inline IFC STEP) or ``file_path`` (server-side path
871	    readable by the FastAPI process) must be supplied. ``slug`` names the new
872	    shadow-model row; ``link_name`` is the host-side display name for the
873	    auto-created ``link_model`` element. Both have sensible defaults so a
874	    minimal request just sends the IFC bytes.
875	    """
876	
877	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
878	    file_text: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="fileText")
879	    file_path: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="filePath")
880	    slug: str = Field(default="ifc-import", min_length=1, max_length=128)
881	    link_name: str = Field(default="Linked IFC", alias="linkName")
882	
883	
884	@api_router.post("/models/{host_id}/import-ifc")
885	async def import_ifc_to_shadow_link(
886	    host_id: UUID,
887	    body: ImportIfcBody,
888	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
889	    hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub),
890	) -> dict[str, Any]:
891	    """FED-04: import an IFC file as a brand-new shadow bim-ai model + auto-
892	    create a ``link_model`` row in the host pointing at it.
893	
894	    Round-trip: parse IFC → ``authoritativeReplay_v0`` command bundle →
895	    apply to a fresh ``ModelRecord`` in the same project → run
896	    ``createLinkModel`` against the host. The shadow model is independent
897	    from then on (host edits never reach back into it; the host treats its
898	    elements as read-only renderable context per FED-01).
899	    """
900	
901	    from bim_ai.engine import (
902	        try_apply_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_v0,
903	        try_commit,
904	    )
905	    from bim_ai.export_ifc import build_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_sketch_v0
906	
907	    # Resolve host first so we can mirror its project_id onto the shadow.
908	    host_row = await load_model_row(session, host_id)
909	    if host_row is None:
910	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Host model not found")
911	
912	    # Read the IFC text. The endpoint accepts either inline text or a path.
913	    if body.file_text is not None:
914	        step_text = body.file_text
915	    elif body.file_path is not None:
916	        try:
917	            with open(body.file_path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
918	                step_text = fh.read()
919	        except OSError as exc:
920	            raise HTTPException(
921	                status_code=400, detail=f"Cannot read IFC file: {exc}"
922	            ) from exc
923	    else:
924	        raise HTTPException(
925	            status_code=400,
926	            detail="import-ifc requires either fileText or filePath in the request body",
927	        )
928	
929	    sketch = build_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_sketch_v0(step_text)
930	    if sketch.get("available") is not True:
931	        raise HTTPException(
932	            status_code=400,
933	            detail={
934	                "reason": "ifc_replay_unavailable",
935	                "ifcReason": sketch.get("reason"),
936	            },
937	        )
938	
939	    # 1. Create the shadow model row in the host's project.
940	    shadow_id = uuid4()
941	    shadow_doc: Document = Document(revision=1, elements={})  # type: ignore[arg-type]
942	    ensure_internal_origin(shadow_doc)
943	    ensure_sun_settings(shadow_doc)
944	
945	    # 2. Apply the replay bundle in-memory.
946	    ok, replayed_doc, applied_cmds, _viols, code = try_apply_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_v0(
947	        shadow_doc, sketch
948	    )
949	    if not ok or replayed_doc is None:
950	        raise HTTPException(
951	            status_code=400,
952	            detail={"reason": "ifc_replay_failed", "code": code},
953	        )
954	
955	    # 3. Persist the shadow model.
956	    shadow_row = ModelRecord(
957	        id=shadow_id,
958	        project_id=host_row.project_id,
959	        slug=body.slug,
960	        revision=replayed_doc.revision,
961	        document=document_to_wire(replayed_doc),
962	    )
963	    session.add(shadow_row)
964	    try:
965	        await session.flush()
966	    except IntegrityError:
967	        await session.rollback()
968	        raise HTTPException(
969	            status_code=409,
970	            detail="Shadow model slug already exists for this project — pass a unique 'slug'",
971	        ) from None
972	
973	    # 4. Build a createLinkModel command and apply it to the host.
974	    suggested_position = {"xMm": 0.0, "yMm": 0.0, "zMm": 0.0}
975	    host_doc = Document.model_validate(host_row.document)
976	    create_link = {
977	        "type": "createLinkModel",
978	        "name": body.link_name,
979	        "sourceModelId": str(shadow_id),
980	        "positionMm": suggested_position,
981	        "rotationDeg": 0.0,
982	        "originAlignmentMode": "origin_to_origin",
983	    }
984	    try:
985	        host_ok, new_host_doc, _cmd, host_viols, host_code = try_commit(host_doc, create_link)
986	    except Exception as exc:
987	        await session.rollback()
988	        raise HTTPException(
989	            status_code=400, detail=f"createLinkModel failed: {exc}"
990	        ) from exc
991	    if not host_ok or new_host_doc is None:
992	        await session.rollback()
993	        raise HTTPException(
994	            status_code=409,
995	            detail={
996	                "reason": host_code,
997	                "violations": [v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for v in host_viols],
998	            },
999	        )
1000	
1001	    # The new link_model element id is the only one missing from doc_before.
1002	    new_link_ids = set(new_host_doc.elements.keys()) - set(host_doc.elements.keys())
1003	    if len(new_link_ids) != 1:
1004	        await session.rollback()
1005	        raise HTTPException(
1006	            status_code=500,
1007	            detail="Internal: createLinkModel did not produce exactly one new element",
1008	        )
1009	    link_element_id = next(iter(new_link_ids))
1010	
1011	    # Persist the host. Keep the undo-stack record so the import is undoable.
1012	    host_row.document = document_to_wire(new_host_doc)  # type: ignore[assignment]
1013	    host_row.revision = new_host_doc.revision
1014	    await session.commit()
1015	
1016	    # Broadcast the host's delta so connected clients pick up the link.
1017	    try:
1018	        await hub.publish(
1019	            host_id,
1020	            {
1021	                "type": "delta",
1022	                "modelId": str(host_id),
1023	                "revision": new_host_doc.revision,
1024	            },
1025	        )
1026	    except Exception:
1027	        # Hub failures must not roll back the import.
1028	        pass
1029	
1030	    return {
1031	        "linkedModelId": str(shadow_id),
1032	        "linkElementId": link_element_id,
1033	        "suggestedLinkPosition": suggested_position,
1034	        "appliedReplayCommandCount": len(applied_cmds),

1044	class ImportDxfBody(BaseModel):
1045	    """FED-04: payload for ``POST /api/models/{host_id}/import-dxf``.
1046	
1047	    Either ``file_path`` (server-side path readable by the FastAPI process)
1048	    must be supplied. ``level_id`` names the host level the underlay is
1049	    attached to. ``origin_mm`` / ``rotation_deg`` / ``scale_factor`` let the
1050	    caller place the linework; defaults centre on the project origin with
1051	    no rotation.
1052	    """
1053	
1054	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
1055	    file_path: str = Field(alias="filePath")
1056	    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
1057	    name: str = Field(default="DXF Underlay")
1058	    origin_mm: dict[str, float] | None = Field(default=None, alias="originMm")
1059	    rotation_deg: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="rotationDeg")
1060	    scale_factor: float = Field(default=1.0, alias="scaleFactor", gt=0)

1062	
1063	@api_router.post("/models/{host_id}/import-dxf")
1064	async def import_dxf(
1065	    host_id: UUID,
1066	    body: ImportDxfBody,
1067	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
1068	    hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub),
1069	) -> dict[str, Any]:
1070	    """FED-04: parse a DXF file and materialise a ``link_dxf`` element.
1071	
1072	    The route reads the file at ``body.file_path``, runs the ``ezdxf``
1073	    parser, then dispatches a single ``createLinkDxf`` engine command on
1074	    the host. Returns the new ``link_dxf`` element id so the frontend can
1075	    open ManageLinksDialog with the new entry highlighted.
1076	    """
1077	
1078	    from pathlib import Path as _Path
1079	
1080	    from bim_ai.dxf_import import parse_dxf_to_linework
1081	
1082	    host_row = await load_model_row(session, host_id)
1083	    if host_row is None:
1084	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Host model not found")
1085	
1086	    dxf_path = _Path(body.file_path)
1087	    if not dxf_path.is_file():
1088	        raise HTTPException(
1089	            status_code=400, detail=f"DXF file not found at filePath: {body.file_path}"
1090	        )
1091	
1092	    try:
1093	        linework = parse_dxf_to_linework(dxf_path)
1094	    except Exception as exc:
1095	        raise HTTPException(
1096	            status_code=400, detail=f"DXF parse failed: {exc}"
1097	        ) from exc
1098	
1099	    host_doc = Document.model_validate(host_row.document)
1100	    if body.level_id not in host_doc.elements or not isinstance(
1101	        host_doc.elements[body.level_id], LevelElem
1102	    ):
1103	        raise HTTPException(
1104	            status_code=400, detail="levelId must reference an existing Level on the host model"
1105	        )
1106	
1107	    create_cmd = {
1108	        "type": "createLinkDxf",
1109	        "name": body.name,
1110	        "levelId": body.level_id,
1111	        "originMm": body.origin_mm or {"xMm": 0.0, "yMm": 0.0},
1112	        "rotationDeg": float(body.rotation_deg),
1113	        "scaleFactor": float(body.scale_factor),
1114	        "linework": linework,
1115	    }
1116	    try:
1117	        ok, new_doc, _cmds, viols, code = try_commit_bundle(host_doc, [create_cmd])
1118	    except Exception as exc:
1119	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"createLinkDxf failed: {exc}") from exc
1120	    if not ok or new_doc is None:
1121	        raise HTTPException(
1122	            status_code=409,
1123	            detail={
1124	                "reason": code,
1125	                "violations": [v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for v in viols],
1126	            },
1127	        )
1128	
1129	    new_link_dxf_ids = [
1130	        eid
1131	        for eid in set(new_doc.elements.keys()) - set(host_doc.elements.keys())
1132	        if getattr(new_doc.elements[eid], "kind", None) == "link_dxf"
1133	    ]
1134	    if len(new_link_dxf_ids) != 1:
1135	        raise HTTPException(
1136	            status_code=500,
1137	            detail="Internal: createLinkDxf did not produce exactly one new link_dxf element",
1138	        )
1139	    link_element_id = new_link_dxf_ids[0]
1140	
1141	    host_row.document = document_to_wire(new_doc)  # type: ignore[assignment]
1142	    host_row.revision = new_doc.revision
1143	    await session.commit()
1144	
1145	    try:
1146	        await hub.publish(
1147	            host_id,
1148	            {
1149	                "type": "delta",
1150	                "modelId": str(host_id),
1151	                "revision": new_doc.revision,
1152	            },
1153	        )
1154	    except Exception:
1155	        pass
1156	
1157	    return {
1158	        "linkedElementId": link_element_id,
1159	        "lineworkCount": len(linework),
1160	    }

1200	@api_router.post("/models/{model_id}/bundles")
1201	async def apply_bundle_route(
1202	    model_id: UUID,
1203	    body: CommandBundleRequest,
1204	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
1205	    hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub),
1206	) -> dict[str, Any]:
1207	    """CMD-V3-01: submit a CommandBundle; returns BundleResult.
1208	
1209	    mode='dry_run' (default) — validates without mutating.
1210	    mode='commit'            — commits if no blocking advisories fire.
1211	    HTTP 409 on revision_conflict or assumption_log_required / malformed.
1212	    """
1213	    from datetime import UTC, datetime
1214	
1215	    from bim_ai.engine import compute_delta_wire, diff_undo_cmds
1216	    from bim_ai.routes_deps import delete_redos, document_to_wire
1217	    from bim_ai.tables import UndoStackRecord
1218	
1219	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
1220	    if row is None:
1221	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
1222	
1223	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
1224	    mode = body.mode if body.mode in ("dry_run", "commit") else "dry_run"
1225	
1226	    result, new_doc_from_bundle = _apply_bundle(doc, body.bundle, mode, model_id=str(model_id))  # type: ignore[arg-type]
1227	
1228	    # Surface blocking advisory classes as HTTP 409
1229	    _BLOCKING_ADVISORY_CLASSES = {
1230	        "revision_conflict",
1231	        "assumption_log_required",
1232	        "assumption_log_malformed",
1233	        "assumption_log_duplicate_key",
1234	        "direct_main_commit_forbidden",
1235	        "option_routing_not_yet_implemented",
1236	    }
1237	    if not result.applied and result.violations:
1238	        blocking_classes = {v.get("advisoryClass") for v in result.violations}
1239	        if blocking_classes & _BLOCKING_ADVISORY_CLASSES:
1240	            raise HTTPException(
1241	                status_code=409,
1242	                detail={
1243	                    "result": result.model_dump(by_alias=True),
1244	                    "violations": result.violations,
1245	                },
1246	            )
1247	
1248	    if result.applied and result.new_revision is not None and new_doc_from_bundle is not None:
1249	        new_doc = new_doc_from_bundle
1250	        uid = body.user_id or "local-dev"
1251	        doc_before = clone_document(doc)
1252	        undo_cmds = diff_undo_cmds(doc_before, new_doc)
1253	        await delete_redos(session, model_id, uid)
1254	
1255	        session.add(
1256	            UndoStackRecord(
1257	                model_id=model_id,
1258	                user_id=uid,
1259	                revision_after=new_doc.revision,
1260	                forward_commands=body.bundle.commands,
1261	                undo_commands=undo_cmds,
1262	                created_at=datetime.now(UTC),
1263	            )
1264	        )
1265	
1266	        wire_doc = document_to_wire(new_doc)
1267	        row.document = wire_doc  # type: ignore[assignment]
1268	        row.revision = new_doc.revision
1269	        await session.commit()
1270	
1271	        delta = compute_delta_wire(doc_before, new_doc)
1272	        try:
1273	            await hub.broadcast_json(
1274	                model_id, {"type": "delta", "modelId": str(model_id), **delta}
1275	            )
1276	        except Exception:
1277	            pass
1278	
1279	    return result.model_dump(by_alias=True)
1280

1286	
1287	
1288	async def websocket_loop(
1289	    websocket: WebSocket,
1290	    model_id: UUID,
1291	    hub: Hub,
1292	    resume_from: int | None = None,
1293	) -> None:
1294	
1295	    sid = str(model_id)
1296	
1297	    async with SessionMaker() as session:
1298	        row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
1299	
1300	    await websocket.accept()
1301	
1302	    if row is None:
1303	        await websocket.close(code=4404)
1304	
1305	        return
1306	
1307	    hub.subscribe(sid, websocket)
1308	
1309	    if resume_from is None:
1310	        doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
1311	        await websocket.send_json(
1312	            {
1313	                "type": "snapshot",
1314	                "modelId": sid,
1315	                "revision": doc.revision,
1316	                "elements": {
1317	                    k: el.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, el in doc.elements.items()
1318	                },
1319	                "violations": violations_wire(doc.elements),
1320	            },
1321	        )
1322	    else:
1323	        replayed = hub.resume(sid, resume_from)
1324	        if replayed is None:
1325	            await websocket.send_json({"type": "RESYNC", "modelId": sid})
1326	        else:
1327	            for payload in replayed:
1328	                await websocket.send_json(payload)
1329	            await websocket.send_json(
1330	                {
1331	                    "type": "replay_done",
1332	                    "modelId": sid,
1333	                    "resumedFrom": resume_from,
1334	                }
1335	            )
1336	
1337	    try:
1338	        while True:
1339	            msg = await websocket.receive_json()
1340	
1341	            mt = msg.get("type")
1342	
1343	            if mt == "presence_update":
1344	                pid = msg.get("peerId")
1345	
1346	                if not isinstance(pid, str) or not pid:
1347	                    continue
1348	
1349	                hub.set_peer_id(websocket, pid)
1350	
1351	                patch = {str(k): v for k, v in msg.items() if k != "type"}
1352	
1353	                hub.touch_presence(sid, pid, patch)
1354	
1355	                await hub.broadcast_presence(sid)
1356	
1357	            elif mt == "presence":
1358	                # legacy noop relay
1359	
1360	                payload = msg.get("payload", {})
1361	
1362	                await hub.broadcast_json(
1363	                    sid, {"type": "presence", "modelId": sid, "payload": payload}
1364	                )
1365	
1366	    except WebSocketDisconnect:
1367	        pass
1368	
1369	    finally:
1370	        hub.unregister(websocket)
1371	
1372	
1373	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1374	# COL-V3-01 — yjs Y-WebSocket collab endpoint
1375	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1376	
1377	
1378	@api_router.websocket("/models/{model_id}/collab")
1379	async def collab_ws(
1380	    websocket: WebSocket,
1381	    model_id: UUID,
1382	) -> None:
1383	    """COL-V3-01: yjs Y-WebSocket endpoint for real-time collab on a model.
1384	
1385	    Relays raw yjs sync + awareness bytes between browser clients multiplexed
1386	    by modelId. Does not interpret CRDT contents — yjs algorithms handle merge
1387	    deterministically on each client.
1388	    """
1389	    orchestrator = get_orchestrator()
1390	    await websocket.accept()
1391	    room = orchestrator.get_room(str(model_id))
1392	    room.join(websocket)
1393	    try:
1394	        while True:
1395	            data = await websocket.receive_bytes()
1396	            await room.broadcast(data, exclude=websocket)
1397	    except WebSocketDisconnect:
1398	        room.leave(websocket)
1399	        orchestrator.remove_empty_rooms()
1400	        logger.info("collab ws disconnect model=%s", model_id)
1401

1500	) -> dict[str, Any]:

@api_router.get("/bootstrap")
async def bootstrap(session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)) -> dict[str, Any]:
    proj_res = await session.execute(select(ProjectRecord))
    projects_out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for p in proj_res.scalars().all():
        mres = await session.execute(select(ModelRecord).where(ModelRecord.project_id == p.id))
        models = [
            {
                "id": str(m.id),
                "slug": m.slug,
                "revision": m.revision,
            }
            for m in mres.scalars().all()
        ]
        projects_out.append({"id": str(p.id), "slug": p.slug, "title": p.title, "models": models})
    return {"projects": projects_out}


class CreateEmptyModelBody(BaseModel):
    slug: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=128)
    # VIE-06: optional template id (e.g. "residential-eu") to seed the model.
    template_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="templateId")


@api_router.post("/projects/{project_id}/models")
async def create_empty_model(
    project_id: UUID,
    body: CreateEmptyModelBody,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    proj = await session.get(ProjectRecord, project_id)
    if proj is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Project not found")
    mid = uuid4()

    if body.template_id:
        if not template_exists(body.template_id):
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Template '{body.template_id}' not found")
        try:
            seed_doc = load_template_snapshot(body.template_id)
        except (FileNotFoundError, LookupError) as exc:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(exc)) from exc
        # Reset revision so the new model starts at 1 regardless of template state.
        seed_doc.revision = 1
    else:
        seed_doc = Document(revision=1, elements={})  # type: ignore[arg-type]

    # KRN-06: every new model has the singleton internal_origin from inception.
    ensure_internal_origin(seed_doc)
    ensure_sun_settings(seed_doc)
    wire = document_to_wire(seed_doc)
    row = ModelRecord(
        id=mid,
        project_id=project_id,
        slug=body.slug,
        revision=seed_doc.revision,
        document=wire,
    )
    session.add(row)
    try:
        await session.commit()
    except IntegrityError:
        await session.rollback()
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=409,
            detail="Model slug already exists for this project",
        ) from None
    return {
        "id": str(mid),
        "projectId": str(project_id),
        "slug": body.slug,
        "revision": row.revision,
        "templateId": body.template_id,
    }


@api_router.get("/templates")
async def list_template_catalog() -> dict[str, Any]:
    """VIE-06: enumerate built-in project templates."""
    rows = list_templates()
    return {
        "templates": [
            {
                "id": t.id,
                "name": t.name,
                "description": t.description,
                "thumbnailUrl": t.thumbnail_url,
            }
            for t in rows
        ]
    }

@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/snapshot")
async def snapshot(
    model_id: UUID,
    expandLinks: bool = False,  # noqa: N803 — wire-format alias
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
    if row is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
    # KRN-06: backfill internal_origin for legacy models that pre-date this WP.
    # Read-only — we don't persist; the next command commit will pick it up.
    ensure_internal_origin(doc)
    ensure_sun_settings(doc)
    elements_wire = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc.elements.items()}
    if expandLinks:
        # FED-01: inline every linked source's elements with provenance markers
        # so renderers can ghost them. Default snapshot omits these to keep the
        # payload small.
        elements_wire = await _expand_host_links(session, doc, elements_wire)
    link_source_revisions = await _resolve_link_source_revisions(session, doc)
    out: dict[str, Any] = {
        "modelId": str(row.id),
        "revision": doc.revision,
        "elements": elements_wire,
        "violations": violations_wire(doc.elements),
    }
    if link_source_revisions:
        # FED-01 polish: per-source current revisions so the UI can render
        # drift badges on pinned links without an extra round-trip.
        out["linkSourceRevisions"] = link_source_revisions
    return out


async def _resolve_link_source_revisions(
    session: AsyncSession, host_doc: Document
) -> dict[str, int]:
    """Look up the current revision of every distinct source UUID referenced
    by a ``link_model`` element in ``host_doc``. Missing sources are omitted
    from the result. Used by the FED-01 drift-badge UI."""

    out: dict[str, int] = {}
    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
        if not isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
            continue
        src_uuid_str = elem.source_model_id
        if src_uuid_str in out:
            continue
        try:
            src_uuid = UUID(src_uuid_str)
        except ValueError:
            continue
        src_row = await load_model_row(session, src_uuid)
        if src_row is None:
            continue
        try:
            src_doc = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
        except Exception:
            continue
        out[src_uuid_str] = int(src_doc.revision)
    return out


async def _expand_host_links(
    session: AsyncSession,
    host_doc: Document,
    host_elements_wire: dict[str, Any],
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """FED-01: resolve every ``link_model`` row's source document from DB and
    pass it through ``expand_links`` to inline transformed source elements.

    Sources are loaded at their pinned revision when set (replayed via the
    undo stack), or at their current revision otherwise. Missing sources are
    skipped silently — the host is still authoritative.
    """

    cache: dict[tuple[str, int | None], Document | None] = {}

    async def _load_source(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
        cache_key = (source_uuid_str, source_rev)
        if cache_key in cache:
            return cache[cache_key]
        try:
            source_uuid = UUID(source_uuid_str)
        except ValueError:
            cache[cache_key] = None
            return None
        src_row = await load_model_row(session, source_uuid)
        if src_row is None:
            cache[cache_key] = None
            return None
        current = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
        if source_rev is None or source_rev == current.revision:
            cache[cache_key] = current
            return current
        # Replay backwards through the undo stack to land at the requested
        # revision (mirrors the diff endpoint's logic).
        try:
            doc_at = await _document_at_revision(session, source_uuid, current, source_rev)
        except HTTPException:
            cache[cache_key] = None
            return None
        cache[cache_key] = doc_at
        return doc_at

    # Pre-load every link's source synchronously (the providers callable in
    # ``expand_links`` is sync; we resolve up-front).
    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
        if isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
            await _load_source(elem.source_model_id, elem.source_model_revision)

    def _provider(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
        return cache.get((source_uuid_str, source_rev))

    return expand_links(host_doc, host_elements_wire, _provider)


async def _document_at_revision(
    session: AsyncSession, model_id: UUID, current: Document, target_rev: int
) -> Document:
    """Reconstruct ``current`` rolled back to ``target_rev`` by replaying
    undo bundles in reverse-revision order. Returns a fresh ``Document``;
    the ``revision`` attribute is informational and may not equal
    ``target_rev`` after the engine bumps the counter — element state is
    what matters for diff.
    """
    if target_rev == current.revision:
        return clone_document(current)
    res = await session.execute(
        select(UndoStackRecord)
        .where(
            UndoStackRecord.model_id == model_id,
            UndoStackRecord.revision_after > target_rev,
            UndoStackRecord.revision_after <= current.revision,
        )
        .order_by(desc(UndoStackRecord.revision_after), desc(UndoStackRecord.id))
    )
    rolling = clone_document(current)
    for entry in res.scalars().all():
        ok, new_doc, _cmds, _viols, _code = try_commit_bundle(rolling, list(entry.undo_commands))
        if ok and new_doc is not None:
            rolling = new_doc
        else:
            raise HTTPException(
                status_code=409,
                detail=(
                    "Cannot reconstruct historical revision — undo replay failed at "
                    f"revision_after={entry.revision_after}"
                ),
            )
    return rolling


@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/diff")
async def model_diff(
    model_id: UUID,
    fromRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803 — wire-format alias
    toRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
    if row is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
    current = Document.model_validate(row.document)

    to_rev = toRev if toRev is not None else current.revision
    from_rev = fromRev if fromRev is not None else max(1, to_rev - 1)

    if from_rev > current.revision or to_rev > current.revision:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=400,
            detail=(
                f"Revision out of range: model is at revision {current.revision} "
                f"(fromRev={from_rev}, toRev={to_rev})."
            ),
        )

    doc_to = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, to_rev)
    doc_from = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, from_rev)

    elements_from = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_from.elements.items()}
    elements_to = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_to.elements.items()}

    diff = compute_element_diff(elements_from, elements_to)
    return {
        "modelId": str(model_id),
        "fromRevision": from_rev,
        "toRevision": to_rev,
        **diff,
    }


@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/summary")
async def model_summary(
    model_id: UUID,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
    if row is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
    return {
        "modelId": str(model_id),
        "revision": doc.revision,
        "summary": compute_model_summary(doc),
    }


@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/validate")
async def validate_model_snapshot(
    model_id: UUID,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
    if row is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
    viols = violations_wire(doc.elements)
    err_ct = sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("severity") == "error")
    block_ct = sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("blocking") is True)
    return {
        "modelId": str(model_id),
        "revision": doc.revision,
        "violations": viols,
        "summary": compute_model_summary(doc),
        "checks": {"errorViolationCount": err_ct, "blockingViolationCount": block_ct},
    }


@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/evidence-package")
async def evidence_package(
    model_id: UUID,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
    if row is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
    viols = violations_wire(doc.elements)
    err_ct = sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("severity") == "error")
    block_ct = sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("blocking") is True)
    kinds: dict[str, int] = {}
    for e in doc.elements.values():
        k = getattr(e, "kind", "?")
        kinds[k] = kinds.get(k, 0) + 1

    schedules = [{"id": sid, "name": doc.elements[sid].name} for sid in list_schedule_ids(doc)]

    pv_index = plan_view_wire_index(doc)

    payload: dict[str, Any] = {
        "format": "evidencePackage_v1",
        "generatedAt": datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),
        "modelId": str(model_id),
        "revision": doc.revision,
        "elementCount": len(doc.elements),
        "countsByKind": kinds,
        "summary": compute_model_summary(doc),
        "validate": {
            "violations": viols,
            "checks": {"errorViolationCount": err_ct, "blockingViolationCount": block_ct},
        },
        "exportLinks": export_link_map(model_id),
        "planViews": pv_index,
        "expectedScreenshotCaptures": expected_screenshot_captures(
            [str(p["id"]) for p in pv_index]
        ),
        "recommendedCapture": [
            {
                "id": "cockpit_plan_3d",
                "workspaceLayoutPreset": "split_plan_3d",
                "planPresentation": ["default", "opening_focus", "room_scheme"],
                "regions": [],
            },
            {
                "id": "schedule_focus",
                "workspaceLayoutPreset": "schedules_focus",
                "planPresentation": ["opening_focus"],
            },
            {
                "id": "sections_and_plan",
                "workspaceLayoutPreset": "split_plan_section",
            },
            {
                "id": "sheet_placeholder",
                "note": "Use sheet canvas coordination layout when wired",
                "workspaceLayoutPreset": "coordination",
            },
        ],
        "scheduleIds": schedules,
        "roomDerivationPreview": room_derivation_preview(doc),
        "roomDerivationCandidates": room_derivation_candidates_review(doc),
        "typeMaterialRegistry": merged_registry_payload(doc),
        "hint": "Use Playwright to capture PNG alongside this JSON per spec §8.3 / §14 Phase A. CI attaches artifacts alongside this bundle.",
        "sheetRasterNote": (
            "Sheet SVG/PDF exports are deterministic server-side. "
            "`GET …/exports/sheet-print-raster.png` returns a deterministic 128×112 RGB8 **print-surrogate** PNG "
            f"(`{SHEET_PRINT_RASTER_PRINT_SURROGATE_CONTRACT_V2}`: 96px viewport layout stamp + 16px titleblock "
            "metadata strip + SVG UTF-8 salt) for CI correlation — not a true raster of the sheet SVG; use Playwright "
            "captures for baseline PNG diffing."
        ),
    }
    plan_ids = sorted(eid for eid, e in doc.elements.items() if isinstance(e, PlanViewElem))
    first_plan = plan_ids[0] if plan_ids else None
    levels_sorted = sorted(
        (e for e in doc.elements.values() if isinstance(e, LevelElem)),
        key=lambda le: (le.elevation_mm, le.id),
    )
    fl0 = levels_sorted[0].id if levels_sorted else None
    payload["planProjectionWireSample"] = resolve_plan_projection_wire(
        doc,
        plan_view_id=first_plan,
        fallback_level_id=fl0,
        global_plan_presentation="default",
    )
    payload["advisorSeveritySummary_v1"] = {
        "format": "advisorSeveritySummary_v1",
        "error": sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("severity") == "error"),
        "warning": sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("severity") == "warning"),
        "info": sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("severity") == "info"),
    }
    payload["semanticDigestSha256"] = evidence_package_semantic_digest_sha256(payload)
    digest = str(payload["semanticDigestSha256"])
    payload["semanticDigestPrefix16"] = digest[:16]
    payload["suggestedEvidenceArtifactBasename"] = f"bim-ai-evidence-{digest[:16]}-r{doc.revision}"
    payload["suggestedEvidenceBundleFilenames"] = {
        "format": "evidenceBundleFilenames_v1",
        "evidencePackageJson": f"{payload['suggestedEvidenceArtifactBasename']}-evidence-package.json",
    }
    payload["recommendedPngEvidenceBackend"] = "playwright_ci"
    payload["svgRasterBackendAvailable"] = True
    payload["deterministicSheetEvidence"] = deterministic_sheet_evidence_manifest(
        model_id=model_id,
        doc=doc,
        evidence_artifact_basename=str(payload["suggestedEvidenceArtifactBasename"]),
        semantic_digest_sha256=digest,
        semantic_digest_prefix16=str(payload["semanticDigestPrefix16"]),
    )
    payload["deterministic3dViewEvidence"] = deterministic_3d_view_evidence_manifest(
        model_id=model_id,
        doc=doc,
        evidence_artifact_basename=str(payload["suggestedEvidenceArtifactBasename"]),
        semantic_digest_sha256=digest,
        semantic_digest_prefix16=str(payload["semanticDigestPrefix16"]),
    )
    payload["deterministicPlanViewEvidence"] = deterministic_plan_view_evidence_manifest(
        model_id=model_id,
        doc=doc,
        evidence_artifact_basename=str(payload["suggestedEvidenceArtifactBasename"]),
        semantic_digest_sha256=digest,
        semantic_digest_prefix16=str(payload["semanticDigestPrefix16"]),
    )
    payload["deterministicSectionCutEvidence"] = deterministic_section_cut_evidence_manifest(
        model_id=model_id,
        doc=doc,
        evidence_artifact_basename=str(payload["suggestedEvidenceArtifactBasename"]),
        semantic_digest_sha256=digest,
        semantic_digest_prefix16=str(payload["semanticDigestPrefix16"]),
    )
    payload["evidenceClosureReview_v1"] = (
        merge_committed_png_fixture_baselines_into_evidence_closure_review_v1(
            merge_server_png_byte_ingest_into_evidence_closure_review_v1(
                evidence_closure_review_v1(
                    package_semantic_digest_sha256=digest,
                    deterministic_sheet_evidence=payload["deterministicSheetEvidence"],
                    deterministic_3d_view_evidence=payload["deterministic3dViewEvidence"],
                    deterministic_plan_view_evidence=payload["deterministicPlanViewEvidence"],
                    deterministic_section_cut_evidence=payload["deterministicSectionCutEvidence"],
                ),
                REDACTED,
                REDACTED,
            )
        )
    )
    payload["evidenceDiffIngestFixLoop_v1"] = evidence_diff_ingest_fix_loop_v1(
        payload["evidenceClosureReview_v1"]
    )
    payload["evidenceReviewPerformanceGate_v1"] = evidence_review_performance_gate_v1(
        payload["evidenceDiffIngestFixLoop_v1"]
    )
    payload["evidenceLifecycleSignal_v1"] = evidence_lifecycle_signal_v1(
        package_semantic_digest_sha256=digest,
        suggested_evidence_artifact_basename=str(payload["suggestedEvidenceArtifactBasename"]),
        evidence_closure_review=payload["evidenceClosureReview_v1"],
    )
    payload["agentEvidenceClosureHints"] = agent_evidence_closure_hints()
    payload["bcfTopicsIndex_v1"] = bcf_topics_index_v1(doc)
    payload["agentReviewActions_v1"] = agent_review_actions_v1(
        doc=doc,
        deterministic_sheet_evidence=payload["deterministicSheetEvidence"],
        deterministic_3d_view_evidence=payload["deterministic3dViewEvidence"],
        deterministic_plan_view_evidence=payload["deterministicPlanViewEvidence"],
        deterministic_section_cut_evidence=payload["deterministicSectionCutEvidence"],
        violations=viols,
        evidence_closure_review=payload["evidenceClosureReview_v1"],
    )
    payload["agentBriefCommandProtocol_v1"] = agent_brief_command_protocol_v1(
        doc=doc,
        proposed_commands=[],
        validation_violations=viols,
    )
    payload["evidenceAgentFollowThrough_v1"] = evidence_agent_follow_through_v1(
        model_id=model_id,
        doc=doc,
        package_semantic_digest_sha256=digest,
        suggested_evidence_artifact_basename=str(payload["suggestedEvidenceArtifactBasename"]),
        bcf_topics_index=payload["bcfTopicsIndex_v1"],
        deterministic_sheet_evidence=payload["deterministicSheetEvidence"],
        deterministic_3d_view_evidence=payload["deterministic3dViewEvidence"],
        deterministic_plan_view_evidence=payload["deterministicPlanViewEvidence"],
        deterministic_section_cut_evidence=payload["deterministicSectionCutEvidence"],
        violations=viols,
        evidence_closure_review=payload["evidenceClosureReview_v1"],
        evidence_diff_ingest_fix_loop=payload["evidenceDiffIngestFixLoop_v1"],
    )
    payload["artifactUploadManifest_v1"] = artifact_upload_manifest_v1(
        model_id=model_id,
        suggested_evidence_artifact_basename=str(payload["suggestedEvidenceArtifactBasename"]),
        package_semantic_digest_sha256=digest,
        evidence_closure_review=payload["evidenceClosureReview_v1"],
    )
    follow_raw = payload.get("evidenceAgentFollowThrough_v1")
    ref_res = (
        follow_raw.get("evidenceRefResolution_v1")
        if isinstance(follow_raw, dict)
        and isinstance(follow_raw.get("evidenceRefResolution_v1"), dict)
        else None
    )
    payload["agentGeneratedBundleQaChecklist_v1"] = agent_generated_bundle_qa_checklist_v1(
        brief_protocol=payload["agentBriefCommandProtocol_v1"],
        validate=payload["validate"],
        schedule_ids=payload["scheduleIds"],
        export_links=payload["exportLinks"],
        deterministic_sheet_evidence=payload["deterministicSheetEvidence"],
        deterministic_plan_view_evidence=payload["deterministicPlanViewEvidence"],
        evidence_diff_ingest_fix_loop=payload["evidenceDiffIngestFixLoop_v1"],
        evidence_review_performance_gate=payload["evidenceReviewPerformanceGate_v1"],
        evidence_ref_resolution=ref_res,
    )
    payload["agentBriefAcceptanceReadout_v1"] = agent_brief_acceptance_readout_v1(
        doc=doc,
        brief_protocol=payload["agentBriefCommandProtocol_v1"],
        qa_checklist=payload["agentGeneratedBundleQaChecklist_v1"],
        artifact_upload_manifest=payload.get("artifactUploadManifest_v1"),
        validation_violations=viols,
    )
    payload["evidenceBaselineLifecycleReadout_v1"] = evidence_baseline_lifecycle_readout_v1(
        evidence_closure_review=payload["evidenceClosureReview_v1"],
        evidence_diff_ingest_fix_loop=payload["evidenceDiffIngestFixLoop_v1"],
        evidence_review_performance_gate=payload["evidenceReviewPerformanceGate_v1"],
    )
    payload["agentReviewReadoutConsistencyClosure_v1"] = (
        agent_review_readout_consistency_closure_v1(
            readout_brief_acceptance=payload.get("agentBriefAcceptanceReadout_v1"),
            readout_bundle_qa_checklist=payload.get("agentGeneratedBundleQaChecklist_v1"),
            readout_merge_preflight=None,
            readout_baseline_lifecycle=payload.get("evidenceBaselineLifecycleReadout_v1"),
            readout_browser_rendering_budget=None,
            closure_hints=payload["agentEvidenceClosureHints"],
        )
    )
    payload["v1AcceptanceProofMatrix_v1"] = build_v1_acceptance_proof_matrix_v1(doc)
    payload["v1CloseoutReadinessManifest_v1"] = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    payload["prdAdvisorMatrix_v1"] = build_prd_blocking_advisor_matrix()
    scheme_elem = next(
        (e for e in doc.elements.values() if hasattr(e, "kind") and e.kind == "room_color_scheme"),
        None,
    )
    payload["roomColorSchemeOverrideEvidence_v1"] = build_room_color_scheme_override_evidence_v1(
        scheme_elem
    )
    payload["roomColourSchemeLegendEvidence_v1"] = roomColourSchemeLegendEvidence_v1(doc)
    payload["sheetProductionBaseline_v1"] = sheetProductionEvidenceBaseline_v1(doc)
    payload["evidencePackageDigestInvariants_v1"] = evidence_package_digest_invariants_v1(payload)
    return payload

# Schedule table route
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@api_router.get(
    "/models/{model_id}/schedules/{schedule_id}/table",
    response_model=None,
)
async def schedule_derived_table(
    model_id: UUID,
    schedule_id: str,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
    fmt: Annotated[str, Query(alias="format")] = "json",
    columns: Annotated[str | None, Query(alias="columns")] = None,
    include_schedule_totals_csv: Annotated[bool, Query(alias="includeScheduleTotalsCsv")] = False,
) -> dict[str, Any] | PlainTextResponse:
    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
    if row is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
    try:
        payload = derive_schedule_table(doc, schedule_id)
    except ValueError as exc:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from exc
    if fmt.strip().lower() == "csv":
        export_payload = payload
        if columns and columns.strip():
            wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
            if wanted:
                export_payload = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
        csv_body = schedule_payload_to_csv(
            export_payload,
            include_totals_csv=include_schedule_totals_csv,
        )
        safe = "".join(ch for ch in schedule_id if ch.isalnum() or ch in ("-", "_")) or "schedule"
        return PlainTextResponse(
            csv_body,
            media_type="text/csv; charset=utf-8",
            headers={"Content-Disposition": f'attachment; filename="{safe}.csv"'},
        )
    out = payload
    if columns and columns.strip():
        wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
        if wanted:
            out = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
    return out
